source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Learn how to use full stops, capital letters, question marks and exclamation marks to demarcate sentences.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This Year 2 pack focuses on recognising sentence boundaries and choosing the right end punctuation.

objective.child_goal: I can build a sentence that starts and ends in the right way.
objective.statement: Use capital letters and sentence-ending punctuation accurately.
misconceptions: Uses a capital letter at the start but forgets the end mark.
Uses full stops for every sentence, including questions.
Adds punctuation after every word rather than at the sentence boundary.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A group of words that makes one complete idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: A sentence tells one complete idea.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A story block with a start gate and end gate.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A mark that shows someone is asking something.
vocabulary.audio_script: A question mark shows the sentence asks something.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A talking character holding a question bubble.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate the idea that a sentence has a clear beginning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Look for the word that opens the sentence gate.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A story path begins at a glowing capital-letter gate.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The sentence path needs a start gate and an end sign.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce sentence punctuation as a boundary marker, not decoration.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A capital letter opens the sentence. A punctuation mark closes it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Words travel along a path from capital gate to punctuation sign.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Where is my red hat? starts with a capital letter and ends with a question mark.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model choosing punctuation from sentence meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: This sentence is asking something, so it needs a question mark.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A question bubble connects to the end mark.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag the capital letter, words and end mark into the sentence path.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise sentence construction with explicit boundary cues.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build one complete idea from start to end.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Word tiles snap into a path with fixed start and end slots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Is this telling, asking or shouting?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose whether the learner chooses punctuation by meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen to what the sentence is doing.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three end signs pulse: full stop, question mark, exclamation mark.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show Pip why this sentence needs a question mark.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to confirm sentence-function understanding.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you teach me which end sign I need?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion offers a wrong end sign and waits for correction.

manipulatives.purpose: Let children build a complete sentence from capital letter to punctuation mark.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners drag word tiles and punctuation signs into start, middle and end slots.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Tap-to-place mode, audio replay and reduced-motion tile highlighting are available.

manipulatives.purpose: Help children diagnose and repair missing or mismatched capital letters and end punctuation in complete sentences.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect a sentence, tap the part that needs repair, then choose a capital letter, full stop, question mark or exclamation mark from a playful tool tray.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Offers keyboard and single-switch navigation, tap-to-replace and sentence audio; static and reduced-motion modes use persistent error markers and focus outlines instead of tool animations.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave full stops and question marks before adding exclamation marks and multi-sentence edits.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build simple telling sentences with a capital letter and full stop.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: familiar noun phrase + verb phrase + start/end boundary slots
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check reading load, vocabulary familiarity and sentence completeness.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish asking sentences from telling sentences.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spoken sentence + three end-mark choices + meaning cue
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Audio should make question intonation clear without giving away the answer visually.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Edit sentence boundaries without scattering punctuation through the sentence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: uncapitalised or unpunctuated sentence + edit targets
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep edits short enough for Year 2 working memory.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide spaced review in short story contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two-sentence story extract + one punctuation repair
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid assessing comprehension and punctuation at the same time in early retrieval.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Offer equivalent practice with less movement and fewer visual effects.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: same sentence functions with static highlight feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: SEND review should check reduced visual clutter and predictable response states.

body.prompt: Build the sentence: the dog runs.

hints: Start with a capital letter.
This sentence is telling, so it ends with a full stop.
question_variants.explanation: The sentence starts with The and ends with a full stop because it is telling something.

body.prompt: Choose the end mark: Where is my red hat

hints: This sentence is asking something.
Questions end with a question mark.
question_variants.explanation: Where is my red hat? is a question, so it needs a question mark.

body.prompt: Fix the sentence: my cat is sleeping

expected_answer.value: My cat is sleeping.

hints: The first word needs a capital letter.
This sentence tells something.
question_variants.explanation: My cat is sleeping. starts with a capital letter and ends with a full stop.

body.prompt: Sentence-path mission 1: build the telling sentence “The dog runs”.
body.target_sentence: The dog runs.
body.supported_interaction: adult_or_peer_may read scan position tiles and record without supplying the punctuation answer
body.visual_route: static start gate, meaning or function card, word/clause chunks and end-mark gate
body.phonological_route: optional oral rehearsal of the complete sentence without requiring speech recording
body.motor_alternative: tap, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, verbal tile order or adult-scribed response can replace dragging and handwriting

gamification.mission: repair a sentence path, asking gate or storybook page
gamification.reward: one calm story-spark for a checked boundary or sentence function
gamification.retry_message: Your correct words and marks stay. Open another clue and continue.

hints: Put the capitalised first word at the start gate.
Keep the words in meaning order and close the statement with a full stop.
question_variants.explanation: The dog runs. begins with a capital letter, keeps a clear word order and ends with a full stop because it is a statement.

feedback.correct: Statement built from capital start to full-stop end gate.
feedback.repair: Keep any correct word sequence, mark WHO/WHAT then WHAT HAPPENS, and add the capital and full stop only at the boundaries.
feedback.boundary_evidence: The dog runs. begins with a capital letter, keeps a clear word order and ends with a full stop because it is a statement.
feedback.support_message: Chunking, pointing, tile order and adult-scribed edits carry equal evidence; speed, handwriting and speech production are not scored.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 sentence-punctuation pack with a deterministic 180-item pilot bank. Three curated variants are preserved alongside candidates covering sentence boundaries, capital starts, full stops, question marks, exclamation marks, statement/question/command/exclamation recognition and construction, word order, proofreading, short story transfer, dictation/application and misconception repair. Exclamation tasks use the Year 2 What/How clause pattern rather than treating every excited utterance as the sentence type. Generated tasks provide word and clause chunking, line focus, static sentence paths, symbol-supported function cues, non-drag interactions and rich boundary-specific feedback without timers or public speed measures. Audio is referenced only for listening and dictation tasks, uses ElevenLabs assets blocked pending human review, and never falls back to browser TTS. Independent English, SEND, narration, safeguarding and renderer review remains required before promotion.
